title: BudgetWise
description: A comprehensive personal finance tracker with automated transaction categorization and deep-dive analytics.
longDescription: BudgetWise was born from a frustration with cluttered, ad-heavy finance apps. The mission was to build a tool that prioritized data integrity and visual clarity. It allows users to track expenses, set granular budgets, and visualize their net worth growth through a precise, editorial interface.

features.title: Real-time Analytics
features.description: Instant processing of transactions with dynamic categorization using custom logic.

features.title: Bank-Grade Security
features.description: End-to-end encryption for all sensitive financial metadata.

features.title: Smart Export
features.description: Generate PDF/CSV reports with a single tap, curated for tax readiness.

features.title: Global Sync
features.description: Seamless multi-device synchronization for real-time data access.

challenges.title: Complex Data Visualization
challenges.problem: Rendering thousands of data points without sacrificing UI responsiveness was the primary hurdle. Standard charting libraries were too heavy and didn't match the design system's specific aesthetic requirements.
challenges.solution: Built a custom lightweight SVG graphing engine from scratch. This allowed for granular control over aesthetics and ensured smooth 60fps interaction on mobile devices.
challenges.quote: Reduced bundle size by 45% by stripping away unused charting modules.

challenges.title: Database Concurrency
challenges.problem: Handling massive simultaneous write operations for shared family accounts caused significant locking issues in the initial PostgreSQL setup.
challenges.solution: Implemented a write-ahead logging strategy and optimized middleware to batch transactions. Migrated to a micro-caching layer to handle read-heavy balance inquiries.
challenges.quote: Improved average API response time from 240ms to 42ms.

title: BookWorm
description: Social reading platform for discovering and sharing books with a focus on high-fidelity typography and distraction-free interaction.
longDescription: BookWorm reimagines the digital reading experience by combining social discovery with editorial design principles. Built to help readers find their next favorite book through curated recommendations and community-driven reviews.

features.title: Smart Recommendations
features.description: Personalized book suggestions based on reading history and community preferences.

features.title: Reading Lists
features.description: Create and share curated book collections with custom categories.

features.title: Social Reviews
features.description: Community-driven reviews with rich text formatting and threaded discussions.

features.title: API-First Architecture
features.description: RESTful API built with Node.js and SurrealDB for flexible data querying.

challenges.title: Graph-Based Recommendations
challenges.problem: Traditional relational queries were too slow for generating recommendations across interconnected user preferences and book metadata.
challenges.solution: Leveraged SurrealDB's graph traversal capabilities to build a recommendation engine that queries relationships between users, books, and genres efficiently.
challenges.quote: Recommendation queries dropped from 800ms to under 50ms.

challenges.title: Real-Time Sync
challenges.problem: Keeping reading progress synchronized across devices while handling offline scenarios gracefully.
challenges.solution: Implemented an event-sourcing pattern with conflict resolution, allowing seamless sync when connectivity is restored.
challenges.quote: Zero data loss across 10k+ sync events in testing.

title: POS + Ecommerce API
description: Modular backend in Rust for point-of-sale and ecommerce, built around a Cargo workspace with strict domain boundaries and RBAC at the edge.
longDescription: A production-grade REST API that unifies point-of-sale and ecommerce operations into a single backend, designed from day one to serve multiple stores from a single deployment. The system is built as a modular monolith: a Cargo workspace splits the domain into isolated crates (identity, inventory, purchasing, sales, core, common) with an api-gateway layer that owns HTTP concerns, dependency injection, tenant resolution and error mapping. The goal was to deliver the maintainability benefits of microservices and the isolation guarantees of multi-tenancy without the operational overhead — strong boundaries, independent compilation, and a single deployable unit.

features.title: Modular Cargo Workspace
features.description: Domain split into independent crates (identity, inventory, purchasing, sales) with explicit dependencies enforced by the Rust compiler.

features.title: RBAC with JWT + Argon2
features.description: Permission-scoped middleware, predefined roles (Admin, Manager, Cashier) and password hashing with argon2.

features.title: SQLx Compile-Time Queries
features.description: Every SQL statement is verified against the live schema at build time, catching drift before it ships.

features.title: Axum API Gateway
features.description: Single HTTP entry point with a shared state DI container, unified error-to-HTTP mapping and typed extractors like CurrentUser.

features.title: UUID v7 Identifiers
features.description: Time-ordered IDs across all aggregates to keep indexes tight and preserve natural write ordering.

features.title: Multi-Tenant Foundation
features.description: Architecture prepared to host multiple stores in one deployment, with tenant context resolved at the edge and flowing through the domain via typed extractors.

challenges.title: Tenant Isolation Without a Fleet of Services
challenges.problem: Running multiple independent stores on a single backend means every query, permission check and cache key has to be tenant-aware — and getting it wrong leaks one client's data into another's dashboard.
challenges.solution: Tenant identity is lifted out of handlers into the request context through Axum extractors, so it's resolved once at the edge and flows through repositories automatically. The workspace layout keeps tenant-scoped logic inside the domain crates, leaving the api-gateway as the single place that enforces the boundary.
challenges.quote: Tenancy as a cross-cutting concern resolved at the edge, not a flag threaded through every function.

challenges.title: Preventing a Monolith From Rotting
challenges.problem: Mixing POS and ecommerce flows in a single codebase risked the classic ball-of-mud: sales logic leaking into inventory, circular dependencies, and a test suite that only runs end-to-end.
challenges.solution: Structured the project as a Cargo workspace where each bounded context is its own crate. The compiler blocks accidental coupling, each module exposes a narrow public API, and the api-gateway is the only place that wires them together through a shared application state.
challenges.quote: Enforced domain boundaries at compile time instead of in code review.

challenges.title: Authorization Without Boilerplate
challenges.problem: A POS system has dozens of fine-grained actions (refund, void, adjust inventory, manage users). Hardcoding checks in every handler is error-prone and scatters policy across the codebase.
challenges.solution: Centralized authentication and permission enforcement into Axum middleware and a CurrentUser extractor. Roles and permissions live in the database, are loaded per-request, and routes declare the permission they require — handlers stay focused on business logic.
challenges.quote: Permission checks became a one-line route annotation.

challenges.title: Safe, Repeatable Database Evolution
challenges.problem: Schema changes and initial data (permissions, roles, default store) had to be reliable across developer machines, CI and production without drift or duplicate rows.
challenges.solution: Adopted SQLx migrations for schema and a dedicated idempotent seed crate for bootstrap data. The seed can run any number of times without producing duplicates, and compile-time query checks guarantee the code matches the current schema.
challenges.quote: Schema, queries and seed data all verified before the binary is produced.
